stories.headline: Namma Metro Starts at 5 AM Today to Support Bengaluru's Big City Events
stories.summary: Breaking its usual Sunday schedule, Bengaluru's Namma Metro is starting services as early as 5 AM today to cater to large city events including the TCS World 10K race, one of Asia's biggest running events. The Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited (BMRCL) also withdrew an earlier notice about a temporary Purple Line suspension, ensuring smooth travel across the city. This is a great example of public transport adapting to serve the city's growing needs.
stories.bigWordDef: Temporarily stopping or pausing a service — like when your school cancels classes for a holiday.
stories.thinkQuestion: Bengaluru often struggles with traffic. What other creative ideas could the city try to encourage more people to use public transport like the Metro?
stories.sourceQuote: Breaking away from the traditional 7 am Sunday start, trains will start at 5 am on May 10.

stories.headline: NASA's Curiosity Rover Survived a 6-Day Mars Rock Crisis — and Won
stories.summary: In an unprecedented challenge, NASA's Curiosity rover accidentally lifted an entire 13-kilogram rock off the Martian surface while drilling on April 25 — and the rock got stuck to its drill sleeve for nearly six days! Engineers back on Earth tried several creative techniques before finally freeing the rover on May 1 by tilting, rotating, vibrating, and spinning the drill bit. Curiosity, which has been roaming Mars since 2012 — far beyond its original two-year mission — continues to make history.
stories.bigWordDef: Using repeated striking or hammering force — like how Curiosity's drill pounds into rocks to break them open for samples.
stories.thinkQuestion: The Curiosity team had to solve a brand-new problem on a planet 300 million kilometres away, and they couldn't go there to fix it. How would you think through solving a problem when you can't see or touch what's broken?
stories.videoQuery: NASA Curiosity rover Mars rock drill stuck 2026
stories.sourceQuote: Finally, on May 1, they tilted and rotated the drill while spinning the bit and, after a few tries, the rock came loose, breaking into smaller parts as it hit the ground.

stories.headline: Record Heatwave Scorches South Asia, Pushing Temperatures Past 45°C
stories.summary: A deadly, record-breaking heatwave is sweeping across South Asia, with India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh seeing temperatures soar to between 45–50°C in some areas. India's meteorological chief warned of four to five extra heatwave days along the east coast and in Gujarat, with some areas seeing temperatures 3–5°C above normal. Scientists say climate change will make such extreme heat events more frequent and intense in coming decades.
stories.bigWordDef: A heatwave is a long stretch of unusually hot weather that stays dangerously above normal temperatures for several days in a row, making it unsafe to be outside.
stories.thinkQuestion: If heatwaves in South Asia are getting worse due to climate change, what are some creative ways communities — including yours in Bengaluru — could stay cool and safe?
stories.videoQuery: South Asia heatwave climate change 2026 explained
stories.sourceQuote: A record-breaking, deadly heatwave sweeping South Asia has pushed temperatures to dangerous highs, disrupting daily life for hundreds of millions.

stories.headline: Middle East Conflict Pushes Global Food Prices Up for Third Month in a Row
stories.summary: The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) reported that global food prices rose in April for the third consecutive month, driven partly by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz that are pushing up fertilizer costs. Prices of cereals, vegetable oil, and meat all increased, though the FAO expects global cereal supplies to hold through 2026 as production is forecast to rise 6% year-on-year. The crisis is adding pressure to food security for millions of people worldwide.
stories.bigWordDef: A disruption is something that interferes with normal activities and stops things from working the way they should — like a conflict blocking a shipping route that many countries depend on.
stories.thinkQuestion: When a conflict in one part of the world makes food more expensive everywhere else, what does that tell us about how connected our world really is?
stories.videoQuery: global food prices rising 2026 Middle East explained
stories.sourceQuote: Global food prices rose in April for the third straight month, the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said on Friday, as conflict in the Middle East fuelled global supply concerns.

infographic.caption: Curiosity's Marathon on Mars 🤖
infographic.fact: Curiosity landed on Mars in August 2012 · Its mission was originally planned for just 2 years · As of 2026, it has been exploring for over 13 years · It has drilled hundreds of rock samples · The 'Atacama' incident (rock stuck to drill) was the first of its kind ever · Curiosity has travelled over 32 km across Gale Crater
infographic.question: If Curiosity's original mission was 2 years, how many times longer has it actually operated so far (as of 2026)?
